LAW AND MOTION 302, APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT. PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO INCLUDE A MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, WHICH IS REQUIRED UNDER CRC 3.1113. (SEE ALSO CRC 3.1114.) PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ALSO DOES NOT DESCRIBE THE CATEGORIES OF PROPERTY ADEQUATELY TO ALLOW DEFENDANT TO IDENTIFY THE SPECIFIC PROPERTY SOUGHT TO BE ATTACHED. (SEE CCP 484.020(E).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

Application For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SCOTT HANDELMAN, 925.280.2500, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ROSS MADDEN, ESQ., 415.567.1212, FOR DEFENDANT LORETTA BOZUNG.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: Application For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Plaintiff's application for right to attach is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff has failed to include a memorandum of points and authorities, which is required under CRC 3.1113. (See also CRC 3.1114.) Plaintiff's application for right to attach also does not describe the categories of property adequately to allow Defendant to identify the specific property sought to be attached. (See CCP 484.020(e).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment By Court
Off Calendar Apr-16-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Apr-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SCOTT F. HANDELMAN, ESQ., 925.280.2500, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ROSS MADDEN, ESQ., 415.567.1212, FOR DEFENDANT LORETTA BOZUNG.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT LORETTA BOZUNG's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is granted. Defendant has met the requirements to set aside the default under CCP 473.5. Defendant has shown that she lacked actual notice which was not caused by avoidance or inexcusable neglect. Relief was promptly sought. Plaintiff has not shown good cause for oral testimony. Rule of Court 3.1306. Defendant shall file her response to the Complaint within 5 days of entry of order. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
